title: Statistics :: Immigration New Zealand
content:
Find statistics on Immigration New Zealand’s migration data and the Migration Data Explorer tool.

## Migration Data Explorer
The Migration Data Explorer is an interactive tool that allows you to explore immigration data over time.
You can use the tool to:
- view data on migrants in New Zealand, visa applications, and decisions
- explore trends over time
- break data down by visa type, nationality, or year
- filter results to focus on specific groups
- download the data for your own analysis or reporting.
It replaces the annual Migration Trends report and is updated regularly. To protect personal information, all figures use random rounding to base 3. This in line with Stats NZ best practice. This means numbers may differ slightly from previously published data.
Migration Data Explorer — Ministry of Business, Innovation & Employment
Data confidentiality principles and methods report — Stats NZ

## Statistic files for download
Here you can find statistics on visas, refugees, arrivals and departures. The data shows how visa applications are processed and decided, who is coming to New Zealand and why.
- These statistics show how residence visa applications progress through the immigration system. Resident visa applications decided These statistics show how many resident visa applications were approved or declined. They are broken down by: financial year month of decision application criteria (the visa settings or rules the application was assessed under) nationality, and application sub stream (for example, business/skilled, or international/humanitarian). Statistics — Resident decisions by financial year (pdf, 2.2 MB) Resident visa applications accepted These statistics show how many resident visa applications were accepted for processing. They are broken down by: financial year nationality, and application sub stream (for example, business/skilled, or international/humanitarian). Statistics — Residence applications accepted (pdf, 543 KB) Occupation and region of employment These statistics show how many resident visa applications were approved or declined in each financial year. They are broken down by: occupation of the applicant, and region the applicant was employed in. Statistics — Occupation and region for resident principal applicants (pdf, 1.2 MB) Residence applications on hand These statistics show information on residence applications waiting to be decided (on hand). They are broken down by: financial year application category application criteria, and nationality. Statistics — Residence applications on hand by month (pdf, 447 KB) Returning resident visa applications These statistics show how many returning resident visa applications were approved or declined. They are broken down by: financial year month of decision application criteria (the visa settings or rules the application was assessed under) nationality, and application sub stream (for example, business/skilled, or international/humanitarian). Statistics — Returning resident visa applications (pdf, 882 KB)
These statistics show how residence visa applications progress through the immigration system.

#### Resident visa applications decided
These statistics show how many resident visa applications were approved or declined.
They are broken down by:
- application criteria (the visa settings or rules the application was assessed under)
- application sub stream (for example, business/skilled, or international/humanitarian).

#### Resident visa applications accepted
These statistics show how many resident visa applications were accepted for processing.
They are broken down by:
- application sub stream (for example, business/skilled, or international/humanitarian).

#### Occupation and region of employment
These statistics show how many resident visa applications were approved or declined in each financial year.
They are broken down by:
- occupation of the applicant, and
- region the applicant was employed in.

#### Residence applications on hand
These statistics show information on residence applications waiting to be decided (on hand).
They are broken down by:
- application category
- application criteria, and

#### Returning resident visa applications
These statistics show how many returning resident visa applications were approved or declined.
They are broken down by:
- application criteria (the visa settings or rules the application was assessed under)
- application sub stream (for example, business/skilled, or international/humanitarian).
- These statistics show a summary of New Zealand’s refugee and protection programmes. They show refugee and protection claims as well as refugee quota arrivals, broken down by: regions of settlement nationality, and financial year. The statistics also provide information on the Refugee Family Support Category and Community Organisation Refugee Sponsorship. They are broken down by: invitations to apply, and nationality. INZ refugee statistics pack for April 2026 (pdf, 520 KB)
These statistics show a summary of New Zealand’s refugee and protection programmes.
They show refugee and protection claims as well as refugee quota arrivals, broken down by:
- regions of settlement
The statistics also provide information on the Refugee Family Support Category and Community Organisation Refugee Sponsorship. They are broken down by:
- invitations to apply, and
- These statistics show information on student visas and first-time students. Student visas These statistics show how many student visa applications were approved or declined. They are broken down by: application criteria (the visa settings or rules the application was assessed under) application substream (application category) nationality, and financial year. Statistics — Student visa applications decided (pdf, 868 KB) First-time students These statistics show information on first-time students, broken down by: when students receive their first visa, and nationality. It also includes the number of first-time students changing from student visas to work or residence visas. Statistics — First-time students (pdf, 327 KB)
These statistics show information on student visas and first-time students.
These statistics show how many student visa applications were approved or declined.
They are broken down by:
- application criteria (the visa settings or rules the application was assessed under)
- application substream (application category)
These statistics show information on first-time students, broken down by:
- when students receive their first visa, and
It also includes the number of first-time students changing from student visas to work or residence visas.
- These statistics show how visitor visa applications are decided over time. They are broken down by: application criteria decision outcome nationality, and financial year. Statistics — Visitor applications decided (pdf, 1.2 MB)
These statistics show how visitor visa applications are decided over time.
They are broken down by:
- application criteria
- These statistics provide information on Accredited Employer Work Visas, work visa applications and Recognised Seasonal Employer workers. Accredited Employer Work Visas (AEWV) The AEWV data report shows how many AEWV were approved or decided. These statistics are broken down by: nationality occupation, and sector. AEWV — Data report (xlsx, 127 KB) Work visa applications The 'Work applications decided' statistics show how many work visas were approved or decided. They are broken down by: application criteria (the visa settings or rules the application was assessed under) nationality, and financial year. Statistics — Work applications decided (pdf, 1.5 MB) Recognised Seasonal Employer workers The Recognised Seasonal Employer (RSE) statistics show the number of RSE arrivals. They also show the number of onshore RSE workers, broken down by: region, nationality, and gender. Statistics — Recognised Seasonal Employer (RSE) arrivals (pdf, 280 KB) RSE — Data report (xlsx, 69 KB)
These statistics provide information on Accredited Employer Work Visas, work visa applications and Recognised Seasonal Employer workers.

#### Accredited Employer Work Visas (AEWV)
The AEWV data report shows how many AEWV were approved or decided.
These statistics are broken down by:

#### Work visa applications
The 'Work applications decided' statistics show how many work visas were approved or decided.
They are broken down by:
- application criteria (the visa settings or rules the application was assessed under)

#### Recognised Seasonal Employer workers
The Recognised Seasonal Employer (RSE) statistics show the number of RSE arrivals.
They also show the number of onshore RSE workers, broken down by:
- Limited visas are reported separately, as they apply across multiple visa types. This includes visitor, study, and work categories. These statistics show how many limited purpose visa applications were approved or declined. They are broken down by: financial year decision outcome nationality, and application criteria (the visa settings or rules the application was assessed under). Statistics — Limited purpose applications by financial year (pdf, 572 KB)
Limited visas are reported separately, as they apply across multiple visa types. This includes visitor, study, and work categories.
These statistics show how many limited purpose visa applications were approved or declined.
They are broken down by:
- application criteria (the visa settings or rules the application was assessed under).
- These statistics show border movement data, including: arrivals by month and visa type, and departures of non New Zealand passport holders by nationality. Statistics — Arrivals by month and visa type (pdf, 174 KB) Statistics — Departures of all people not travelling on New Zealand passports (pdf, 555 KB)
These statistics show border movement data, including:
- arrivals by month and visa type, and
- departures of non New Zealand passport holders by nationality.
